page: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dadv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. This CPU provides 48 vCPUs derived from 24 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The processor configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server type includes 2835 GB of bundled local storage and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk attachments. It does not include any GPU accelerators, relying entirely on the host CPU for computational tasks.

Benchmark data indicates strong overall performance. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng ranks in the top 10%, while multi-core performance falls in the top 25%. Geekbench multi-core scores also place the server in the top 10% tier. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is strong, though large uncached read and write operations perform at an average level. For specialized workloads, the server demonstrates strong capabilities in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations. However, a notable performance tradeoff is observed in large-scale LLM inference; while text generation for a small 135M model is in the top 10%, text generation for a large 70B model falls into the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48ads_v6 offers a balanced resource density of compute, memory, and high-capacity local storage. This makes it a viable option for workloads requiring a mix of processing power and local disk space without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. Recommended workloads include relational databases, multi-threaded decompression tasks, software compilation pipelines, and general-purpose web serving.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48ads_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dadv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 x86_64 processor with 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and a 3.7 GHz clock speed. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of RAM and 2835 GB of local storage, with no GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and multi-core Geekbench scores, alongside strong database operations and multi-threaded decompression. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks remains average, and text generation for large 70B LLM models is poor. This server provides a balanced resource density, making it qualitatively cost-effective for workloads such as database hosting, software compilation, and general-purpose applications requiring substantial local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring a 48-vCPU AMD EPYC processor, 192 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core and multi-core CPU performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48ads_v6 is a 48-vCPU AMD EPYC virtual machine with 192 GB RAM and 2835 GB local storage, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine combining 48 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and high-capacity local storage.

Equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory and 2835 GB of local storage
Delivers top-tier single-core and multi-core CPU benchmark performance
Shows strong performance in database operations and software compilation
